colorNotes: Body is white (#fff) with near-black text (rgb(51,51,51)). Dark sections (hero, approach, Imogen, footer) use rgb(13,13,13) — near-black. CTAs are white text on dark bg with no border-radius (0px). Primary accent appears to be green (Mechanical Orchard brand green — visible in logo/highlights, not captured as computed color but present in imagery).
fontNotes: Two-font system: FAM Aime Regular (custom serif, used for H1, H2, CTAs — editorial/literary feel) + Inter Variable (body/UI sans-serif). H1 at ~74px desktop.

sections.gist: Large animated scrolling text: 'If it's not working, do something different. We rapidly rewrite what's working today into a modern form that's ready for anything you want it to do tomorrow — with no risk or disruption.'

Three core principles of Mechanical Orchard's methodology: (1) The system in action is the specification, (2) Recreate its behavior exactly in a modern language, (3) Roll increments into production. Dark background section.
sections.fullText:
Three principles
Legacy modernization is the key to staying competitive, secure, and innovative—but too often, these projects derail under the weight of complexity and risk. The result? Disruptions, soaring costs, and unfinished transformations that leave businesses stuck in the past.
Mechanical Orchard is building technology that modernizes systems based on how they behave. Guided by three core principles, it makes modernization safe, fast, and disruption-free.
Have a workload in mind? Try it with us.
The system in action is the specification
Recreate its behavior exactly in a modern language
Roll increments into production

sections.gist: Introduces Imogen platform: end-to-end mainframe modernization platform. Running system = better spec than code. Captures behavioral specification via real data flows; uses it to guide AI code generation and prove equivalence at every step.
sections.fullText:
Meet Imogen®
Imogen is Mechanical Orchard's end-to-end mainframe modernization platform. Its core insight: the running system is a better specification than its code. Imogen captures what a system actually does — through real data flows across component interfaces — and uses that behavioral specification to guide AI code generation and prove equivalence at every step.
Unlike conventional approaches that write code first and test later, Imogen inverts the process: we know what the new system must do before we build it, and we verify it continuously as we go.
Have a workload in mind? Try it with us.

sections.gist: Blog/insights slider with articles: 'The problem of abundance', 'Reflections on the first year of Imogen', 'We rebuilt that COBOL migration calculator', 'Customer Presentation at Google Cloud Next 2026: SulAmérica', 'Nine lives', Carahsoft GSA press release.

Contact CTA section. 'We believe that every company deserves to realize their vision, free of constraints from the past. Our team's technology, and experience can help them move into this evolving version safely, reliably, fearlessly.' CTA: 'Get in touch'.

animLib: GSAP 3.15 (ScrollTrigger + SplitText + Flip) + Lenis smooth scroll + Webflow interactions
animNotes: Multiple GSAP versions loaded (3.15.0 via Webflow CDN, 3.12.5 and 3.12.7 via cdnjs — likely Webflow's bundled vs. custom scripts). Key plugins: ScrollTrigger (scroll-driven animations), SplitText (character/word text animations), Flip (layout transitions), Observer (scroll direction). Lenis provides smooth scroll inertia. Webflow handles base interaction logic. Pattern: scroll-triggered text reveals (SplitText on H1/H2), parallax, and hero slider transitions.

imagery.notes: 1 video on page (likely Vimeo embed in Imogen section — f.vimeocdn.com scripts loaded). Hero uses full-viewport dark background with large serif text, no video/canvas. 30 images across the page (blog thumbnails, logos, team/case study imagery).

platform: Webflow (cdn.prod.website-files.com, Webflow JS bundle, Finsweet attributes)

designObservations.theme: Mixed: white body with dark near-black sections. Not a pure dark-mode site — uses strategic dark sections (hero, principles, Imogen, footer) against white content sections.
designObservations.typography: Editorial serif (FAM Aime Regular) for display/headings — gives literary, high-trust B2B feel. Inter Variable for body/UI. Strong typographic hierarchy.
designObservations.layout: Full-viewport hero slider. Alternating light/dark sections. Split-column contact. Blog/insights carousel (Flickity/Splide). Sticky nav (transparent over dark hero).
designObservations.ctaStyle: Minimal — text links with no border-radius, no pill/button shapes. CTAs styled as text with serif font. 'Contact us', 'Let us show you how', 'Get in touch', 'Learn more'.

colorPalette.brandAccent: Green (Mechanical Orchard brand green — visible in logo, not extracted as computed)

designObservations.positioning: Legacy mainframe modernization — COBOL rewrites using AI + behavioral testing. San Francisco HQ. Verticals: Banking, Insurance, Retail, Public Sector, Automotive. Product: Imogen® platform.
designObservations.tone: Confident, technical, enterprise B2B. Tagline 'Modernize with confidence' — safety/trust-first messaging. 'First principles thinking meets legacy modernization.' 30 years of best practices positioning.
